title: Universal post-implementation reviewer-grade code review for completed task or task block
intent: Run a reviewer-grade post-implementation review on an already completed task or bounded block of tasks to detect subtle defects, regression risk, architectural drift, unsafe patterns, leakage surfaces, weak abstractions, DRY violations, and closure gaps before accepting the work as truly done.

in_scope: All files changed by the target task or bounded task block
Nearby dependent files that could be silently affected by the change
Runtime, data-flow, typing, API, UI, test, and documentation implications
Detection of regressions not caught by nominal happy-path verification
Detection of hidden coupling, duplication, weak abstractions, and premature generalization
Detection of state leakage, authority leakage, data leakage, and boundary violations
Assessment of whether the implementation matches project canon, contracts, and architectural constraints
Assessment of maintainability, replayability, and closure completeness
out_of_scope: Net-new feature expansion unrelated to the reviewed task
Refactors not justified by concrete findings
Broad repo-wide modernization beyond the bounded review target
Performance micro-optimizations unless the reviewed change introduced measurable risk

required: Target TUNG id or bounded list of TUNG ids
Git diff or exact changed-file list
Relevant authority docs, contracts, and canon documents
Verification artifacts already produced by the implementation task
Any known risk notes, temporary compromises, or explicit scope cuts
optional: Screenshots, logs, traces, or benchmark artifacts
Previous audit findings affecting the same surface

review_objectives: Confirm the implementation actually solves the intended problem and only that problem
Find subtle correctness issues that survive standard lint/type/test gates
Find regression vectors in adjacent behavior, state flow, rendering flow, routing flow, data contracts, and build surfaces
Find thin spots where the code works now but is brittle under realistic future change
Find authority drift, architectural bypasses, and sanctioned-surface violations
Find leakage risks: data leakage, state leakage, secret leakage, cross-boundary leakage, CSS/token leakage, event leakage, and abstraction leakage
Find DRY violations, duplication with divergence risk, copy-paste logic, inconsistent naming, and shadow contracts
Find missing tests, weak assertions, false-confidence tests, or coverage gaps around the changed surface
Find documentation drift, stale comments, stale TODOs, stale exports, stale config, and incomplete closure artifacts
Determine whether the work is genuinely closable or still has open engineering risk

checks: Does behavior match the stated task intent and acceptance criteria exactly
Any wrong edge-case behavior, off-by-one logic, fallback bugs, null/undefined hazards, or unhandled branches
Any mismatch between runtime behavior and static types
Any incorrect assumptions about ordering, timing, identity, or environment
Could adjacent routes, components, providers, API consumers, or shared utilities silently break
Any changed contract shape without complete downstream validation
Any behavior that depends on fragile defaults, hidden globals, or implicit state
Any previously passing invariant now only indirectly preserved
Places where implementation barely passes but has poor resilience
Overfitted conditionals or one-off exceptions that will fracture on next extension
Hardcoded assumptions that should have been centralized or asserted
Missing guards around optional inputs, partial data, stale config, or absent authority
Secret/env leakage to client or logs
Internal-only state or authority crossing public boundaries
Unsanctioned imports, raw primitives, raw anchors, raw styles, or bypasses around approved adapters
Token/theme/style bleed across scopes
Event listener, timer, cache, request, or store lifecycle leakage
Cross-tenant or cross-request contamination where applicable
Matches authority docs, contracts, sanctioned surfaces, and project patterns
No accidental new source of truth
No contract drift between code, docs, exports, and tests
No silent boundary expansion beyond approved scope
Any duplicated logic, duplicated constants, duplicated selectors, duplicated mappings, or duplicated validation
Any copy-paste with divergence risk
Abstractions too thin, too broad, or incorrectly placed
Helpers introduced without clear ownership or reuse justification
Missed extraction where duplication is now structural rather than incidental
Do tests validate the real invariant or only implementation trivia
Any missing regression test for the actual bug/risk introduced
Any snapshots that hide semantic failure
Any mocks that make the test unrealistically easy
Any required route/integration/contract coverage still absent
Readability, naming clarity, file placement, ownership clarity
Whether future reviewer can reason about the change without archaeology
Any stale comments, TODO debt, dead exports, dead branches, or confusing structure
Whether rollback, extension, and debugging remain straightforward
Input validation completeness
Output encoding / rendering safety where applicable
Unsafe redirects, unsafe URLs, unsafe serialization, unsafe file or query handling
Permission / auth / visibility regressions if relevant to the touched surface
Acceptance criteria genuinely satisfied
Verification evidence real and relevant
Reviewer-grade pre-closure review completed explicitly
Reports and PROJECT_PROGRESS updated only after closure resolution
No unresolved blocker or hidden FIXME masked as done

required_execution_steps.name: Assemble review target
actions: Collect exact TUNG ids under review
Collect exact changed files
Collect authority docs and prior reports governing the touched surfaces
State explicit bounded review perimeter

required_execution_steps.name: Read changed code line-by-line
actions: Read every changed file fully, not just hunks
Inspect adjacent helpers, registries, contracts, exports, and tests that define true behavior
Trace imports and outward dependencies for silent coupling

required_execution_steps.name: Run reviewer-grade analytical pass
actions: Perform correctness review
Perform regression vector review
Perform boundary/leakage review
Perform DRY/abstraction review
Perform canon/authority compliance review
Perform test quality and missing-test review

required_execution_steps.name: Run bounded automated verification
actions: Run only verification relevant to the touched surface
Use typecheck/lint/tests/build selectively based on real scope
Do not waste heavyweight checks on audit-only review unless required by the reviewed surface
Capture failures and classify as pre-existing, introduced, or ambiguous with evidence

required_execution_steps.name: Produce findings report
actions: List findings by severity: BLOCKER, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, NOTE
For each finding provide file, exact issue, why it matters, evidence, and fix direction
Separate confirmed issues from hypotheses
Separate actual regressions from design opinions

required_execution_steps.name: Issue closure recommendation
actions: Return one of: CLOSE, CLOSE_WITH_NOTES, FOLLOWUP_REQUIRED, REOPEN
Justify the decision using evidence only
If follow-up needed, enumerate exact bounded follow-up tasks

severity_model.BLOCKER: Incorrect behavior, leak, security issue, broken contract, unsanctioned boundary violation, or closure-invalidating defect
severity_model.HIGH: Strong regression risk, brittle implementation, serious missing verification, or architectural drift likely to cause near-term failure
severity_model.MEDIUM: Maintainability issue, weak abstraction, meaningful DRY violation, incomplete guard/test/doc alignment
severity_model.LOW: Minor inconsistency, readability issue, low-risk duplication, cleanup suggestion
severity_model.NOTE: Non-blocking observation or future hardening idea

deliverables: Explicit finding list with severity, evidence, and recommended action
Closure recommendation: CLOSE / CLOSE_WITH_NOTES / FOLLOWUP_REQUIRED / REOPEN
If needed, a bounded follow-up TUNG list derived from findings
PROJECT_PROGRESS update only if closure outcome permits it and only as final administrative step

mandatory_closure_sequence.name: Scope verification
mandatory_closure_sequence.requirement: Confirm the exact reviewed task/block perimeter, changed files, and authority baseline before judging closure.

mandatory_closure_sequence.name: Automated verification
mandatory_closure_sequence.requirement: Run the relevant bounded verification and record exact results, including classification of pre-existing drift.

mandatory_closure_sequence.name: Reviewer-grade pre-closure code review
mandatory_closure_sequence.requirement: Perform explicit human-style reasoning review across correctness, regressions, leaks, DRY, canon compliance, and test sufficiency. This step is mandatory and cannot be replaced by automation.

mandatory_closure_sequence.name: Closure resolution
mandatory_closure_sequence.requirement: Issue a formal recommendation: CLOSE, CLOSE_WITH_NOTES, FOLLOWUP_REQUIRED, or REOPEN, with evidence.

mandatory_closure_sequence.name: PROJECT_PROGRESS update
mandatory_closure_sequence.requirement: Only after closure resolution, update administrative progress artifacts if the outcome permits it.

success_criteria: Every changed file in the target perimeter was read and reviewed, not just diff hunks
At least one explicit pass was done for subtle regressions and leakage vectors
DRY/abstraction quality was assessed, not ignored as stylistic
Tests were evaluated for sufficiency and realism, not just existence
Authority and sanctioned-surface compliance was explicitly checked
Closure recommendation is unambiguous and justified
No PROJECT_PROGRESS update happens before closure resolution
failure_conditions: Review relies only on passing automation without reviewer-grade reasoning
Only diff hunks were reviewed while surrounding code was ignored
No explicit check of canon/authority compliance
No classification of pre-existing vs introduced failures
Closure claimed despite unresolved blocker-grade issues
Administrative progress updated before closure decision

when_to_generate: Any BLOCKER or HIGH finding requiring code changes
Any repeated duplication pattern with structural risk
Any missing regression test on a confirmed defect path
Any authority/contract drift requiring canonical repair
requirements: Each follow-up must cite the exact finding(s) it resolves
Do not bundle unrelated cleanup into one vague task

operator_notes: This is a universal post-task review TUNG and is intended to be reused after any completed implementation task or bounded cluster of tasks.
Use this when you want a real reviewer pass, not ceremonial closure.
Prefer this task before declaring large feature lines closed or before merging risky architectural work.
